channel.title: Rally News — Featured Stories
channel.description: The most uplifting and viral positive news stories, curated daily by Rally News.

item.title: On the trail of a new dinosaur species in Spain’s premier wine region
item.description: The remains found in the village of Igea, in La Rioja, support the hypothesis that a large herbivorous specimen lived there 120 million years ago

item.title: Naked Mole-Rat Queens Emit a Special Scent to Stop Their Rivals From Reproducing, a New Study Suggests
item.description: The strange-looking creatures are one of two known mammals that use a social structure like that of bees and ants. A ruling rodent maintains power over her colony via one key chemical compound, according to new research

item.title: Monkeys Have Geometric Skills On Par With Human Preschoolers, Study Suggests
item.description: If you've ever gone hiking and reached a fork in the trail, you may be confronted with two triangles: one pointing right, one pointing left.

item.title: In a Paris café, terror attack survivors find community: 'People want to tell each other: I am alive'
item.description: After long days attending the trial concerning the Paris attacks of November 13, 2015, survivors and relatives of victims would meet in a bar to comfort one other. They have continued to gather every month, joined by victims of other attacks, to share experiences, advice and, above all, laughter and

item.title: The device detecting the world’s deadliest creature
item.description: Humans miss this subtle sound of danger approaching – but this cheap gadget can sound the mosquito alarm.

item.title: Rocket Report: India's Vikram-1 nears debut flight; AST to become rocket company?
item.description: "We have done everything that could be done to test Vikram-1 on ground."

item.title: Buzz Aldrin Used This Humble Felt-Tip Pen to Save the Apollo II Astronauts. It Just Sold for More Than $850,000
item.description: In July 1969, a quick-thinking Aldrin used the writing instrument to rearm the engine arm circuit of the lunar module "Eagle" after the switch broke off during the moon landing

item.title: Argentina beat England 2-1 to reach World Cup 2026 final
item.description: Argentina will have a chance to become the first team since Brazil in 1962 to win back-to-back World Cups after Lionel Messi, 39, once again dragged his team to victory.

item.title: Spain blanks favored France to advance to FIFA World Cup final
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro score as Spain reaches the FIFA World Cup title match for only the second time and denies 2018 champion France a third straight final appearance

item.title: Spain blanks favored France to advance to FIFA World Cup final
item.description: Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro score as Spain reaches the FIFA World Cup title match for only the second time and denies 2018 champion France a third straight final appearance

item.title: Solar generated record 25% of EU power in June with Germany, Spain and Poland leading the race
item.description: Solar was ahead of coal, gas, nuclear, wind and hydropower.

item.title: Sinner outlasts Zverev to win second straight Wimbledon title
item.description: 24-year-old Jannik Sinner wore down French Open champion Alexander Zverev, securing a 6-7 (7/9), 7-6 (7/2), 6-3, 6-4 win on Sunday. 'You never know how many times you are going to come back,' said the world No. 1. 'I never take it for granted.'

item.title: Sinner outlasts Zverev to win second straight Wimbledon title
item.description: 24-year-old Jannik Sinner wore down French Open champion Alexander Zverev, securing a 6-7 (7/9), 7-6 (7/2), 6-3, 6-4 win on Sunday. 'You never know how many times you are going to come back,' said the world No. 1. 'I never take it for granted.'

item.title: Humans Have a 'Sixth Sense' You've Probably Never Heard Of – And It Appears to Be Key For Mental Health
item.description: We all know that humans have five senses. But a growing body of research shows we have a sixth one that almost nobody talks about – and it may be just as important for our wellbeing as any of the others.

item.title: Humans Have a 'Sixth Sense' You've Probably Never Heard Of – And It Appears to Be Key For Mental Health
item.description: We all know that humans have five senses. But a growing body of research shows we have a sixth one that almost nobody talks about – and it may be just as important for our wellbeing as any of the others.

item.title: Scientists Think Bee Silk Could Be a Surprising New Supermaterial. Here's Why.
item.description: While the world drowns in plastic, researchers are on the hunt for practical materials that are lightweight, tough, and biodegradable.

item.title: Scientists Think Bee Silk Could Be a Surprising New Supermaterial. Here's Why.
item.description: While the world drowns in plastic, researchers are on the hunt for practical materials that are lightweight, tough, and biodegradable.

item.title: Scientists Think Bee Silk Could Be a Surprising New Supermaterial. Here's Why.
item.description: While the world drowns in plastic, researchers are on the hunt for practical materials that are lightweight, tough, and biodegradable.

item.title: Humans Have a 'Sixth Sense' You've Probably Never Heard Of – And It Appears to Be Key For Mental Health
item.description: We all know that humans have five senses. But a growing body of research shows we have a sixth one that almost nobody talks about – and it may be just as important for our wellbeing as any of the others.

item.title: China is catching up to Elon Musk’s reusable rockets
item.description: China's state-owned space company recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch.

item.title: China is catching up to Elon Musk’s reusable rockets
item.description: China's state-owned space company recovered its first orbital rocket booster after launch.

item.title: Which records has France’s Kylian Mbappe broken at FIFA World Cup 2026?
item.description: From individual milestones to national numbers, several records have tumbled in Mbappe's historic World Cup run.

item.title: World First: Scientists Witnessed a Piece of Earth's Oceanic Crust Being Born
item.description: Across 65,000 kilometers (40,400 miles) of the ocean floor, a system of mid-ocean ridges marks the boundaries where Earth's tectonic plates meet.

item.title: Cyborg Cockroaches Could Help Find Survivors of Natural Disasters. New Diving Suits Allow the Insects to Expand Their Search Underwater
item.description: Previously, researchers created electrical implants to control cockroaches' movements for search-and-rescue missions. Now, they've made 3D-printed suits that provide oxygen, allowing the critters to survive submerged for up to three hours

item.title: First-Ever Close-Up Revealed of Earth's Rare 'Minimoon'
Australia space agency has found 'likely source' of mystery space balls
item.description: The agency says that they "appear to be pressure vessels from a space launch vehicle".

item.title: Founder of prominent underground church released from prison in China
item.description: Ezra Jin, founder of Zion Church, lands in the US and reunites with family who are ‘overwhelmed with joy’The founder of one of China’s most prominent underground churches has been released from prison and reunited with his family in the United States.Ezra Jin, the founder of Zion Ch

item.title: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce tie the knot at star-studded Madison Square Garden ceremony
item.description: The wedding between the superstar singer and football player was officiated by actor Adam Sandler

item.title: How a lock-in with Conor McGregor helped Hudson Young take his game to new heights
item.description: The NSW Blues forward spent a wild night with UFC star Conor McGregor in Dublin late last year – but a jiu-jitsu session with his coach was even more memorable.

item.title: As Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce prepare to tie the knot, we look back at their relationship
item.description: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are expected to celebrate their wedding with a party at New York's Madison Square Garden this weekend.

item.title: NASA astronauts spacewalk to fix robotic arm
item.description: Two NASA astronauts stepping outside the International Space Station to fix robotic arm.

item.title: Remote-controlled cockroach swarm can now breathe underwater
item.description: Tiny 3D-printed diving suits allow cockroaches to walk underwater for up to 3 hours with no ill effects, which could enable a cyborg insect swarm to explore disaster zones and perhaps even Mars

item.title: All eyes on Serena Williams as tennis great makes singles comeback at Wimbledon
item.description: Seven-time champion seizes chance ‘to showcase what I do,’ beginning with matchup against 20-year-old Maya Joint

item.title: Bad Bunny: Latin star lights up London with history-making stadium show
item.description: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.

item.title: Harry and Meghan accept offer to stay at royal estate during UK visit
item.description: The royal residence the pair will use has not been made public, but they will also be staying in private accommodation during the trip.

item.title: UN nuclear watchdog says it has access to Iran's nuclear sites after interim peace deal
item.description: "We hope to be there soon," IAEA chief Rafael Grossi said at a news conference in Japan on Friday, according to Reuters.

item.title: ‘My first thought was how gorgeous she looked’: from Insta DMs to real-life romance in Rome
item.description: Sexual-assault survivor Harrison James, 26, and cancer survivor Bella Johnston, 31, first met on Instagram, but the sparks really flew when they finally came face-to-face.

item.title: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes first player to score in six World Cups
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo scored twice in Portugal’s 5-0 win over Uzbekistan

item.title: Iran leaves note about peace in dressing room after Belgium draw
item.description: Iran leave a message saying "may peace, respect and friendship prevail among all nations" following their World Cup group game with Belgium.

item.title: Mysterious whale 'necropolis' discovered at the bottom of the Indian Ocean
item.description: A team of researchers has uncovered hundreds of fossilized whale carcasses off the southwest coast of Australia. Spanning 1,200 kilometers, this is the largest, deepest and oldest cetacean bone graveyard ever observed.

item.title: Catherine, Princess of Wales Questions Digital Screens’ Role in Children’s Lives
item.description: The princess published an essay Friday warning against a childhood “mediated by screens,” days after Britain announced a ban on social media for children under 16.

item.title: How one new telescope is going to change astronomy forever
item.description: Construction of the Deep Synoptic Array is about to start in rural Nevada. It will reveal untold galaxies in stunning detail and help explain how they form and grow

item.title: Astronomers discover another galaxy seemingly devoid of dark matter
item.description: A galaxy appears to be missing the invisible substance thought to hold such objects together, further challenging long-held assumptions about how galaxies form

item.title: Lionel Messi rewrites history books at record sixth World Cup
item.description: Messi scores three exactly 20 years to the day he made his World Cup debut for Argentina.

item.title: U.S. and Iran reach agreement to end war, signing set for Friday
item.description: U.S. and Iranian officials said they had agreed on a framework to end their war, halt the U.S. blockade of Iran and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: This 28-year-old paid off her law school loans selling stickers and coloring books
item.description: Elyse Burns started selling her art on Etsy when she turned 18. Now she's paid off her student loans and did nearly $5 million last year.

item.title: BTS-mania poised to boost South Korea's economy well into the future
item.description: BTS-mania, with all the fan spending and travel, is poised to boost South Korea's economy well into the future

item.title: Venus, Jupiter gather for ‘cosmic kiss’ in rare celestial phenomenon
item.description: The planetary conjunction will be visible to the naked eye, if weather permits.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo Debuts New Song Featuring Robert Smith at Primavera Sound
item.description: Olivia Rodrigo performed her new song "What's Wrong with Me" with Robert Smith at the Primavera Sound festival.

item.title: Google will pay SpaceX $920M per month for compute
item.description: In a statement, a Google representative described the deal as a result of unexpected demand for its recently launched AI products.
